howItWorks.intro: This sea level rise calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Rise projection scaled linearly from 2020 baseline to 2100 target
NOAA scenarios: Low (1ft), Intermediate (2ft), High (4ft), Extreme (6.5ft) by 2100
Storm surge adds 3-15+ feet on top of sea level during hurricanes
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How much will sea levels rise?
faq.answer: NOAA projects 1-6.5 feet of rise by 2100 depending on emissions. The current rate is ~3.6mm/year and accelerating. Even under best-case scenarios, at least 1 foot of rise is locked in from ice already melting.

faq.question: What areas are most at risk?
faq.answer: South Florida, Louisiana Gulf Coast, Chesapeake Bay, NYC metro, and Pacific islands face the highest risk. Any coastal area below 10 feet elevation should plan for eventual flooding impacts.

faq.question: Does this account for storm surge?
faq.answer: No — this shows baseline sea level only. Hurricane storm surge adds 3-15+ feet. A Category 3 hurricane with 3 feet of sea level rise could push water 18+ feet above current sea level in vulnerable areas.

faq.question: Is the Sea Level Rise Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Sea Level Rise Calculator?
sections.body: The Sea Level Rise Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your property elevation, climate scenario, target year, you get instant results including projected rise by target year, safety margin, risk level. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting projected rise by target year right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your property elevation and need to find the right projected rise by target year. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
